$comment: Documented-exception ledger for the parity closed loop. SOURCE OF TRUTH = the shots + specs generated from the design; any visual divergence is a BUG to fix by default. This holds ONLY narrow, documented exceptions, each governed by a doc. `exceptions` (FE-side): the FE intentionally does NOT match the mock — match by screen == entry.screen AND (entry.node == '*' OR node startsWith entry.node) AND (entry.kind == '*' OR entry.kind == finding kind); each needs a `source` citing a doc/owner ruling. `coverageExempt` (kit-side): kit nodes intentionally exempt from data-mx-node coverage. Keep MINIMAL — when in doubt, fix the FE to match the design.

exceptions.reason: The kit models each screen as its own MxScaffold (dashboard/screen); the FE factors the scaffold into the shared, persistent AppShell, so it cannot carry a per-screen ValueKey without rebuilding the whole shell on every tab switch. Chrome it provides (appbar/notifications/quick-review) IS keyed there when the Today tab is active.

The kit puts an app-bar overflow menu (import/export/settings) on the library screen. The FE consolidates those actions into the shared language Drawer (opened from the avatar), so there is no duplicate overflow control; adding one would create two paths to the same actions.

The kit shows an inline expanding search dock in the app bar. The FE routes the search button to a dedicated full-screen /search experience (richer filtering than an inline dock), so there is no in-place search-dock node.

The kit's empty library offers a secondary 'Add words' CTA. The FE data model requires a deck before any word can be added, so the empty state offers only 'Create deck' (empty-deck); an 'Add words' action is impossible with zero decks.

The kit deletes a single card via a confirm dialog launched from the card row. The FE manages cards (edit, hide, delete) inside the flashcard editor, not via a deck-detail row dialog, so there is no per-row delete-cancel/delete-ok here. (Deck deletion — deck-delete-* — IS keyed.)

The kit exposes a 'reset progress' confirm on deck-detail. v1 SRS scheduling is engine-driven (8-box Leitner) with no manual per-card/deck progress reset, so reset-cancel/reset-ok have no FE counterpart.

The kit plays card audio inline from the deck-detail row. The FE plays/generates audio inside the flashcard editor (audioSpeak), keeping the row a compact navigation target, so there is no row-level play-audio control.
The kit offers an in-screen search dock on deck-detail. The FE routes search to the dedicated global /search screen rather than a per-deck inline dock.
The kit's move flow has a separate 'apply' button. The FE move dialog applies immediately when a destination (root or a candidate deck) is tapped, so there is no distinct move-apply step.

The kit opens an add bottom-sheet (add word / sub-deck / import) from the FAB. The FE add FAB pushes the flashcard editor route directly (_addWord → context.push), so there is no intermediate add-menu sheet or its keyed items.

The kit's deck-actions menu keys rename/move/reset/delete as mx-node literals. The FE renders deck actions as a Material bottom-sheet whose items are keyed deckActionRename/deckActionMove/deckActionDelete (not mx-node), and has no reset item (v1), so there is no mx-node:deck-detail/deck-rename literal.
As with deck-rename: the FE deck-actions bottom-sheet keys its move item deckActionMove (Material ListTile), not the kit's mx-node:deck-detail/deck-move literal.

The kit shows current/longest streak on the statistics screen. The FE surfaces streak (current + longest) on the dashboard Today tab where it drives engagement; the statistics screen focuses on library/SRS/accuracy/activity metrics and does not duplicate the streak counters.

The kit's appbar node is the large 'Stats' title. The FE keys statistics/appbar on the header-controls region (the Padding wrapping the current-pair ↔ all-app MxSegmentedControl); the large title is owned by the shell/route, so the appbar key is a proxy for the scope-control header. Intentional, not changed in the parity pass.

The kit's weekly section is 'Time per week (min/day)'. The FE maps this section to a due-forecast bar chart (statsForecastTitle, summary.dueForecast) because there is no time-per-week source in the domain; the head node is the same, the title/content bind to the FE truth, not the kit mock.

The kit's heatmap is a 'Study calendar (last 14 weeks)'. The FE renders an activity heatmap over a 12-week window (statsHeatmapTitle); the window size and wording differ — the FE is the source of truth.

The kit's overview shows total/mastered/due keyed as ov-0/ov-1/ov-2. The FE overview card shows pairs/decks/words (+ a mastered progress bar) and does not key the three stat cells (only overview-head is keyed), so ov-0/1/2 have no FE counterpart and the metric binding differs.

The kit's accuracy block is a donut ('88% / accuracy'). The FE renders a linear progress bar + a percent + statsAccuracyDetail (correct/total), and only when summary.hasReviews. Only accuracy-head is keyed; the body content differs from the kit mock.

The kit puts a user-profile section in settings. MemoX is a local-first app with no user account/identity; the only account surface is the optional Google Drive sync tile, so there is no profile node in settings.

The kit offers a single inline SRS-notification switch. The FE configures study reminders on a dedicated /reminder screen (enable + time + weekdays), which is richer than a single switch, so settings has a reminder navigation row instead of an inline toggle.

Stages 2-5 of NewLearn (and DueReview) reuse the shared W5 game widgets (recall/typing/guess/matching). The in-card controls reveal/check/hint/options/due-next/due-relearn are rendered by those widgets and carry their own game-* node identity, so the study session does not re-key them under study-session/*.
Control provided by the shared W5 typing game widget (game-typing/*), reused by the study session.
Control provided by the shared W5 game widgets, reused by the study session.
The multiple-choice options are provided by the shared W5 guess game widget (game-mc/*), reused by the study session.
DueReview grading (due-next / due-relearn) is provided by the shared recall game widget driving the SRS grade, not by study-session-owned buttons.
The kit has a dedicated resume-session prompt (resume-back/resume-retry). v1 has no mid-session resume prompt; an interrupted session simply re-enters fresh, so there is no resume state.

The kit splits load vs save failures into distinct recovery screens (save-error-back/retry). v1 surfaces any session load/persist failure as a single message state, so there are no separate save-error affordances.

The kit lets you edit a card inline while browsing (edit / edit-cancel / edit-save). Review is a strictly read-only browse that never mutates cards or the schedule (D-007); edits go through the flashcard editor screen, so there is no inline edit dialog.
The kit plays card audio from the review browser. Audio playback/generation lives in the flashcard editor; the review browse is read-only term+meaning, so there is no audio control here.
The kit offers a per-card options menu in review. The read-only browse has no per-card actions, so there is no options control.

The kit has an in-review text-size control. Font scale is a global personalization setting (applied live app-wide from the /theme screen), not a per-browse control, so review has no local text-size affordance.
The kit offers a playback-speed control. v1 auto-play advances on a fixed interval with no speed adjustment, so there is no speed control.
The kit offers a player options menu. v1 auto-play is a minimal term+meaning playback with no per-session options, so there is no options control.
Font scale is a global personalization setting (applied live app-wide from the /theme screen), not a per-player control.

The kit models the play/pause control as an MxFab. The FE renders it as MxIconButton(primary) so the four transport controls (prev / playpause / next / speak) form one consistent icon-button row, rather than a floating action button breaking the row.

The kit's end-state Replay button is the primary variant. The FE pairs Replay(outline) + Close(default), giving Replay secondary emphasis as the optional restart action; kit primary would over-weight it against Close.

The result is a finished-state of the study-session screen, not a separate route; it reuses the study-session MxScaffold and app bar (keyed study-session/appbar), so there is no separate study-result app bar.
The kit shows daily-goal progress on the session result. Goal status is surfaced on the dashboard Today tab; the result reports words + accuracy only.
The kit offers a 'review wrong answers' action on the result. v1 re-queues missed cards within the session itself (D-016) and keeps no post-session wrong list, so there is no review-wrong action.
The kit offers a 'finish later' action. v1 finishes the session in place (continue or to library); there is no defer-session action.
The kit splits SRS finalize failures into later/retry recovery. v1 persists SRS grades automatically as the session runs, so the result has no finalize-later/finalize-retry recovery UI.
The kit matching game has an explicit next control. The FE matching game auto-progresses — a correct pair disappears and the round advances on its own (D-015) — so there is no manual next button.
The kit offers a per-game options menu. The FE matching game is a minimal pair-the-tiles board with no in-game options control.
The kit guess game has a next control. The FE guess game auto-advances on answer (a tap grades and moves on, D-015), so there is no manual next button.
The kit plays prompt audio in the guess game. Audio lives in the flashcard editor; the game prompt is a minimal term display with no audio control.
The kit lets you edit the card from the guess game. Card editing is done in the flashcard editor, not mid-game, so there is no in-game edit control.
The kit recall game has a next control. The FE recall game auto-advances after self-grading (forgot/remembered grade and move on, D-015), so there is no manual next button.
The kit offers a per-game options menu. The FE recall game is a minimal reveal-and-grade card with no in-game options control.
Audio playback lives in the flashcard editor; the recall card is a minimal term/meaning reveal with no audio control.
Card editing is done in the flashcard editor, not mid-game, so there is no in-game edit control.
The kit fill-in game has a next control. The FE typing game auto-advances after a correct check or accept/retry (D-015), so there is no manual next button.
The kit offers a per-game options menu. The FE typing game is a minimal type-the-term card with hint/check/retry/accept controls and no options menu.

The kit has a discrete 'to preview' step. The FE import is a single page: once a source is picked/pasted, the column mapping and a live preview render inline, so there is no separate to-preview navigation step.

The kit splits export into generate then a separate save action. The FE writes the file directly when do-export runs (platform file-save / web download via FileSaveService), so there is no separate save button.
The kit offers a share action for the exported file. v1 export writes the file to disk (or downloads on web) with no OS share-sheet integration, so there is no share control.

The kit has an in-screen time picker with its own done button. The FE uses the platform showTimePicker dialog whose OK/confirm button is OS-provided and not a keyable FE widget, so there is no reminder/picker-done node.

W10 is alpha: the cloud-sync orchestration (CloudSyncService + SyncNow LWW) is built and reachable from a single Sync tile in /settings (keyed account/sync), but there is no dedicated account screen. The full account UI is gated on the GCP/OAuth human gap (client id, Drive API, per-platform config).
No dedicated account screen exists (W10 alpha); sync is a /settings tile, so there is no account app bar.
MemoX is local-first with no user account/identity; there is no profile surface (W10 alpha, GCP human gap).
Sign-in/sign-out UI (signin/signout) is part of the W10 GCP/OAuth human gap; v1 triggers sign-in implicitly via SyncNow (signInRequired) with no standalone auth buttons yet.
The Google-connect control is part of the W10 GCP/OAuth human gap (client id, Drive API enablement, per-platform config), not yet surfaced in the FE.
A standalone sync-now button belongs to the (unbuilt) account screen; v1 runs SyncNow from the /settings sync tile's onTap (account/sync), so there is no separate sync-now control.

The kit's remove view shows two fixed sample rows (pair-0-del / pair-1-del). The FE renders a per-pair delete affordance for every language pair, keyed dynamically by pair id (removeTile-<id>), not by fixed index, so there are no fixed pair-0/pair-1 nodes.

fe preview = flat (a demo surface inside the settings list, deliberately not competing with primary elevated cards), kit = elevated. theme_parity_test.dart reads this exceptionKind:variant + feVariant to assert flat instead of the gen.json elevated.
fe preview content = l10n themePreview / themePreviewBody (real UI copy), kit = 학교 / school / 'Study now' sample copy. The slot bindings follow the FE truth (theme.slots.json), not the kit mock.
fe accent = 3 MxChip (AccentChoice brand/warm/cool, keyed dynamically accent-<name>), kit = 6 literal swatches (accent-0..5). v1 exposes three accent choices, not a six-colour palette.

fe section heads = MxText.title (single-line label), kit = MxSectionHeader. The FE renders all three personalization heads as simple one-line titles (no trailing action), a component-type divergence separate from the accent-head style styleExempt.

fe section heads = MxText.title (single-line label), kit = MxSectionHeader. Component-type divergence (the accent-head styleExempt only documents the card-surface style delta, not the component choice).
fe section heads = MxText.title (single-line label), kit = MxSectionHeader. The FE renders all three personalization heads as simple one-line titles (no trailing action).

fe mode/size = MxSegmentedControl without a literal mx-node key (identity not rolled out); kit = keyed segmented control. Widget is correct; the mx-node identity for the segmented controls is not yet keyed in the FE.

fe mode/size = MxSegmentedControl without a literal mx-node key (identity not rolled out); kit = keyed segmented control. Widget is correct; the mx-node identity for the segmented controls is not yet keyed in the FE.

kit models audio-play as MxIconButton (icon-only volume_up); the FE uses a labelled outline MxButton (Speak) for a clearer affordance in the form, consistent with the design-system button variants. Composition gate only asserts present/absent by key, not component, so this does not fail the gate.

kit models the reminder time as a standalone MxCard (elevated); the FE renders reminder/time as a Container (surface bg + cardRadius) that is the trailing value of a settings-row ListTile, not a full-width standalone card.

kit models reminder/time-edit as a standalone MxIconButton (schedule icon); the FE uses a full ListTile row (tap-to-edit opens showTimePicker) so the whole row is the edit target, not a separate icon button.

kit models export/progress as an MxCard (elevated) transient exporting card (spinner + bar). The FE has no transient exporting UI (export() runs synchronously) and keys export/progress on a bare Text that shows the RESULT message (exportCopied / exportSavedTo(path) / transferError) after the export completes. This is why the parity test is Template B (present/absent), not Template A (a widget<MxCard> cast on this Text would crash).

kit models import as a 5-step wizard (source→mapping→preview→dup-warning→done, each a separate screen). The FE is a single scrolling screen driven by _rows/_result — an intended v1 divergence that still satisfies the business rules (BR-2 preview-before-write via an inline 5-row preview; soft-dup non-blocking, D-025).
kit models the column pick as an MxIconButton (opens a picker sheet). The FE uses a DropdownButtonFormField<int> (keyed with the same ValueKey) to choose the term column inline.
as map-term-pick: the FE meaning-column pick is a DropdownButtonFormField<int>, not the kit MxIconButton picker.

kit shows a pre-import dup-warning callout (ActionCallout: N cards already exist — import anyway?). The FE applies soft-dup (D-025): it counts duplicates and reports them in the post-import result line (importDone(imported, duplicates)) without a blocking pre-import callout — consistent with AC-2 (warn on confirm, still add).

kit shows the filter-chip row only in query states (results/filtered/no-results), hidden on empty-recent. The FE renders the filter row ALWAYS (a stable bar at the top of the body), so filters is present on the recent screen too — a deliberate layout choice. The node IS keyed (search/filters); this documents the always-shown divergence so the composition gate does not try to assert it absent on empty-recent.
kit keys a separate clear/close button (search/clear) in the app bar. The FE renders clear as the TextField suffixIcon inside search/dock, so there is no separate clear node.
kit keys a back button (search/back). The FE uses the Material AppBar default leading provided by Navigator, so there is no separate keyed back node.

kit scopes the search control into library/context (the with-data body, shown only when the library has decks). The FE renders the search button in a stable toolbar that is drawn in EVERY state (loaded/empty/error/loading), so search-btn is present outside the with-data body — a deliberate always-visible toolbar. The node IS keyed (library/search-btn); this documents why it is excluded from the state-composition gate universe (it is not state-discriminating).

kit scopes the sort control into library/context (the with-data body). The FE renders the sort button in the always-visible toolbar (drawn in every state), so sort-btn is not scoped to the with-data body. The node IS keyed (library/sort-btn); documents why it is excluded from the state-composition gate universe. (The sort SHEET itself uses a showModalBottomSheet of ListTiles, not keyed sort-0..3 — a separate identity-rollout gap.)

kit derives the create control only in the loaded state (and scopes search/sort into library/context). The FE renders create in the always-visible toolbar, so it also appears in empty/error/loading. This is the same toolbar-always divergence as search-btn/sort-btn; create additionally has a styleExempt for its icon-only-vs-labelled form. The node IS keyed (library/create); documents why it is excluded from the state-composition gate universe.

$styleExemptDoc: Per-node STYLE divergences for tool/parity/spec_diff.mjs (bg/color/font/r). Match by screen == entry.screen AND (entry.node == '*' OR node startsWith entry.node) AND (entry.field == '*' OR entry.field == finding field). Keep MINIMAL — fix the FE to the kit by default; each entry needs a `source`.

styleExempt.reason: The empty state uses a 3-tier emphasis: primary 'Add word' / outline 'New sub-deck' / ghost 'Import'. The FE renders the secondary 'new sub-deck' CTA as MxButtonVariant.outline; the kit models it as a ghost button, so its border/fill differ.
The kit's add-language submit is r:12; the FE drawer uses a Material FilledButton (default stadium radius). Fill matches (primary).
The kit's add-language submit label is 20px; the FE Material FilledButton uses the standard label size (labelLarge, 15px).

The kit's library/create is an icon-only primary '+' button (bg:primary, r:24). The FE uses a labelled ghost 'Create deck' action in the toolbar (clearer affordance) and keeps the high-emphasis primary create in the empty state (library/empty-deck), so create's fill/colour/size intentionally differ.

The kit shows the reminder time as a 24px element inside a pill row. The FE uses a standard settings ListTile row (consistent with every other settings row) with the time value rendered as a surface chip (reminder/time), so the row title keeps the standard list-title size.
The kit's time-edit is a fully-rounded pill row (r:9999). The FE uses a standard rectangular settings ListTile for consistency with the rest of the settings screens.

The kit wraps only the accent section in a surface card (bg:surface r:20) while mode/size heads are flat. The FE renders all three personalization section heads as uniform flat labels (MxText.title), so accent-head has no card fill/radius.
The kit's check button is r:12; the FE typing game uses a Material FilledButton (default stadium radius). Fill/colour match.

The kit's hint button is r:12 / 20px / primary-strong; the FE typing game uses a Material OutlinedButton (stadium radius, primary outline, labelLarge 15px). A plain game button, not the kit's chip treatment.

The kit's reveal button is r:12 with a 20px label. The FE recall game uses a Material FilledButton (default stadium radius + labelLarge 15px); fill/colour match (primary/surface), the radius + label size are the kit's button treatment not adopted by the plain game button.

The kit's 'Forgot' grade button is an MxButton. The FE recall game uses a Material OutlinedButton (default stadium radius + labelLarge); the outline/secondary emphasis matches the kit's intent, the radius + label size are the kit's button treatment not adopted by the plain game button.

The kit's 'Remembered' grade button is an MxButton. The FE recall game uses a Material FilledButton (default stadium radius + labelLarge); fill/emphasis (filled primary) matches the kit's intent, the radius + label size are the kit's button treatment not adopted by the plain game button.

The kit's guess options are large pill choice buttons with text-coloured labels at 24px. The FE uses standard Material OutlinedButtons (primary outline + label) for the choices; fill/radius are pill-equivalent, the label colour + 24px size are the kit's richer treatment.
The kit's option labels are 24px; the FE OutlinedButton uses the standard label size (labelLarge, 15px).

The kit styles the repeat-mode scope selector as a surface chip (bg:surface r:20). The FE uses a standard Material DropdownButton for scope selection, which the flat exporter reads as an unfilled control.
The kit's continue button label is 20px; the FE uses the standard MxButton label size (labelLarge, 15px). Fill/colour now match (primary).

The kit's secondary result action uses an accent-soft fill with primary-strong text. The FE uses the MxButton secondary variant (primary-soft / on-primary-soft) — the design system has no accent-soft button variant; the emphasis (primary continue + soft library) matches the kit.
The kit's play/pause icon is 26px (between icon-size-md 22 and lg 28). The FE uses the MxIconButton primary icon size (MxIconSize.md, 22px); 26 is not an icon-size token.

The kit's review browse uses circular icon nav buttons (r:9999, 24px). The FE uses labelled Back/Continue MxButtons (control radius, labelLarge) for clearer affordance, consistent with the design-system button variants.

The kit's review browse uses circular icon nav buttons (r:9999, 24px). The FE uses labelled Back/Continue MxButtons (control radius, labelLarge) for clearer affordance, consistent with the design-system button variants.
The kit's continue/next button label is 20px; the FE uses the design-system's standard MxButton label size (labelLarge, 15px) consistently across all action buttons.
MxSwitch is custom-painted and not introspectable by the flat exporter; its styling follows the MxSwitch design-system component, not the kit's primary-fill pill.
The kit's export button label is 20px; the FE uses the design-system's standard MxButton label size (labelLarge, 15px) consistently across all action buttons.

MxSwitch is a custom-painted control (not a Material/Container/TextField box), so the flat style exporter reads no bg/color/font/r for it. Its styling follows the MxSwitch design-system component (covered by its own widget smoke test), not the kit's surface-sunken pill.

The kit renders 'add secondary meaning' as a surface chip (bg:surface, primary-strong, r). The FE editor uses a ghost MxButton for this low-emphasis secondary action, consistent with the design-system button variants used across forms.

The kit renders the audio control as a circular surface chip (bg:surface, r:9999). The FE editor uses an outline MxButton ('Speak') consistent with the design-system button variants, so its fill/radius differ.

Statistics is a tab body under the shared AppShell chrome — it has no per-screen large title bar. The statistics/appbar node is keyed on the screen's top control (the current-pair ↔ all-app scope toggle), whose own segmented-control style is intentionally not the kit's 30/800 title.

statistics/screen is a tab-body layout container (Column) with no own fill; its background is the page bg supplied by the shell Scaffold. The flat exporter captures the first descendant box (the scope toggle) instead, so the screen-bg comparison is not meaningful here.

The kit's first preview text run is a muted caption (text-tertiary). The FE preview card leads with a normal-weight title (MxText.title, text colour) followed by body/sample controls, so the first text run is `text` not `text-tertiary`.
The kit soft-duplicate banner primary CTA is MxButton primary; the FE renders dup-add as a ghost MxButton (low-emphasis inline banner action next to dup-view), so its fill/emphasis differ.

$identityRolloutDoc: Kit nodes present in contracts/<screen>.gen.json but NOT yet keyed with ValueKey(mx-node:...) in lib/** — the identity-rollout backlog (fe_node_usage exempts these from MISSING). Distinct from `exceptions` (intentional FE↔kit divergences): these are simply not-keyed-yet, or a structural difference noted in the screen contract. Ratchet target: move nodes OUT of here into real FE keys over time. Regenerate after keying: remove the keyed segment.
